source.description: A six-panel raster contact sheet generated for the locked May 23 Sketcha.day backfill slug, then cropped into step frames and the finished master.
source.notes: The sheet must keep one folded paper airplane in the same three-quarter view, with crease and shadow details introduced before the final.

process_strategy: The airplane is built from a center spine, triangular wings, folded nose, crease lines, paper edge thickness, and a soft shadow before the final only darkens keeper lines and adds restrained graphite tone.

final_elements.name: center spine
final_elements.description: A long diagonal fold line that controls the airplane's direction.

final_elements.name: main wing triangles
final_elements.description: Two broad triangular wing planes meeting at the center spine.

final_elements.name: folded nose
final_elements.description: A pointed nose formed by the front wing edges.

final_elements.name: rear wing edges
final_elements.description: Back edges that show the plane's folded paper layers.

final_elements.name: crease lines
final_elements.description: Light interior fold lines that explain the paper construction.

final_elements.name: paper thickness and tabs
final_elements.description: Small edge lines and overlaps showing the folded paper layers.

final_elements.name: soft graphite shadow
final_elements.description: A simple shadow under the plane to lift it from the page.

frames.visible_job: Places the long diagonal spine and two simple triangular wing guides.
Connects the front wing edges into a clean folded nose.
Adds the rear wing edges and trims the silhouette into a paper-airplane shape.
Adds light fold creases inside the already shaped wings.
Adds small paper-edge overlaps, a few tabs, and a soft shadow under the plane.

final_step.visible_job: Only darkens the keeper contours, clarifies the existing creases, paper overlaps, nose, wings, and soft shadow.
allowed_changes: small cleanup marks on existing creases and folded edges

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
